Description: ESP32-S3 WiFi/BT module - NO PSRAM variant
Notes: MUST be the N8 (quad flash, no PSRAM) variant. config.h uses GPIO35/36/37/38 as DEBUG_GPIO test points - the R8/R16/N8R8/N16R8 variants use GPIO33-37 internally for octal PSRAM, which would conflict. Symbol/footprint from Espressif's official KiCad library (github.com/espressif/kicad-libraries, install via KiCad Plugin & Content Manager).

Description: USB-C receptacle - native USB for flash/JTAG/console + bench power
Notes: D+/D- to ESP32-S3 GPIO20/GPIO19 (fixed native-USB pins). This board carries no mains, so USB-C VBUS can double as a bench power input; field power comes from J-PWR instead (see Power section) since USB-C is a poor fit for a sealed outdoor enclosure.
Required for UFP (device) detection on USB-C. Standard value, any 1% 0805 resistor.
Place close to J1 on the D+/D- lines before they reach the module.
Ties EN high per Espressif reference design; module resets while EN is low.
Optional but recommended for a clean power-on reset edge.
Pulls GPIO0 (strapping pin) to GND when held - needed to force download mode if esptool's automatic USB-JTAG reset ever fails in the field.
Keeps GPIO0 high (normal boot) when SW6 is not pressed.
One per VDD pin group per Espressif hardware design guidelines; place close to module pins.
Tight decoupling directly at module VDD pins.
Board is entirely SELV/low-voltage - powered from an external 5V wall-wart/supply feeding this terminal block, not from mains directly. No isolated AC-DC, fuse, or MOV needed on this board at all.

Description: Power-input OR-ing Schottky diodes (USB-C VBUS vs. J-PWR)
Notes: One in series with USB-C VBUS, one in series with J-PWR, diode-ORed onto the shared 5V rail so plugging in USB for bench flashing never backfeeds the field supply (or vice versa).
Steps the 5V rail down to 3.3V for the module + control outputs + LED chain. Use TI's datasheet application circuit for L1/C1-C4 sizing.
Value per TI TPS563201 datasheet application circuit; substitute any equivalent shielded 2.2uH/>2A inductor.
Per TPS563201 datasheet application circuit.
Bulk cap on the 5V rail feeding U3 and the LED chain.
Bulk cap on the 3.3V logic rail.

Description: Logic-level N-channel MOSFET - low-side driver for external SSR/relay module control input
Notes: Replaces the on-board triac stage entirely - no mains-side parts on this board. GPIO drives the gate; drain sinks the external SSR/relay module's control '-' terminal to GND when on, source to GND. Buffering through a MOSFET (rather than driving the module's opto input straight off a 3.3V GPIO) gives a full 5V logic swing, which is safely within spec for the widest range of off-the-shelf 3-32VDC SSR/relay modules - some are marginal at bare 3.3V.
Between the ESP32 SSR GPIO and each 2N7000 gate.
Holds each gate low (output off) while the GPIO is undriven/tri-stated during boot.

Description: External SSR/relay module control-output terminal block (per channel)
Notes: Carries only the 5V control-side signal to an external SSR/relay module (control '+' wired to the module directly from the 5V rail off-board, control '-' from this terminal through the MOSFET drain). No mains anywhere on this board.

Section: Control output (not stocked here - purchase separately per channel)
Notes: Reference part only, not part of this board's BOM. Mains L/N wiring runs directly from the incoming supply through each module's load terminals to the pump/valve/light, physically bypassing this PCB entirely - see the on-board-SSR variant if you want everything on one board instead. Verify the chosen module's input trigger current/voltage and its own mains-side fusing/MOV needs, which live in the external wiring harness, not on this board.
Low-voltage (3.3V-side) signal.
Protects FLOAT1_SENS_GPIO for the long outdoor sensor run called out in the doc.
Between J7 and the GPIO/TVS node.
Forms an RC low-pass with R13 against noise pickup on the long outdoor run.

Firmware drives one WS2812 chain of 6 pixels (Pump1, Pump2, Valve1, Light1, Float, Sys) off GPIO10 via the led_strip RMT driver - NOT 4 discrete LED+resistor pairs as in the design doc's indicative BOM. Chain DIN->DOUT in firmware pixel order 0-5 (config.h *_LED_PIX defines).
One per WS2812B per datasheet recommendation.
Between GPIO10 (LED_DATA_GPIO) and the first WS2812B DIN - standard practice to limit ringing/inrush on the data line.

KiCad_Footprint: Custom - THT PC-pin, panel bushing through enclosure faceplate (no standard KiCad footprint; build a 3-pad THT footprint per datasheet)
Notes: One per output channel (Pump1, Pump2, Valve1, Light1). Common -> 3V3, throw A -> SW_A GPIO, throw B -> SW_B GPIO. Firmware already configures SW_A/SW_B as GPIO_PULLDOWN_ONLY inputs, so no external pull resistors are needed - center/AUTO position reads both low.
Wired to RESET_BTN_GPIO, idle-high via internal pull-up (GPIO_PULLUP_ONLY in firmware, no external resistor needed). Firmware requires a 5s hold to clear the leak lockout and esp_restart().
Breaks out the 9 DEBUG_GPIO_* task-timing test points (config.h) + GND for a logic analyzer during bring-up. Omit for a production board, or gate behind DEBUG_GPIO_ENABLE.
